original: Leave
What ship are you looking to trade for?
You ask her if they have any ships for sale... specifically ships you would actually want. Anything as rusty as the Nova itself is bound to be a hard no.

The dockmaster brushes a sweep of cyan hair aside in annoyance. “‘Course we got the goods. Though you’ll have to trade in whatever you’re flying for it. This hangar’s too tight for anybody to use it as a glorified garage, I don’t care how shiny their scales.”

original: Back

It’s hard to tell who’s in charge among the dozens of Raskvel scampering around the hangar bay - at least until you see the gigantic glowing techno-wrench ominously swaying around. In its wake a couple of the mechanical shorties sprawl away with a yelp, and you can just about make out the call of: “Fuck off, you scrappy bastards! This is fer sale! Not fixin’!”

This time when you look for the dockmaster, your eyes are already scanning the horizon for purple and silver. For now she seems to have been left alone, stood next to a giant propped-up wrench and eyeing a dangerously-sparking module. You can practically hear the clicking of her tongue as she yanks out a wire and lets it gutter out into nothing.
Dockmaster
